enrichment.short_description: Open-source, high-performance time series database for storing and querying metrics, logs, and traces at scale.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: VictoriaMetrics: open-source time series database, Prometheus-compatible, for monitoring at scale. Free self-hosted core, Enterprise and Cloud available.

features.title: Prometheus compatibility
features.description: Works as a drop-in replacement for Prometheus, supporting existing PromQL-based dashboards and alerting rules.

features.title: VictoriaLogs and VictoriaTraces
features.description: Extends the core metrics database with companion products for log storage and distributed tracing.

features.title: High-performance querying
features.description: Optimized storage and query engine designed to process millions of metrics per second efficiently.

features.title: Elastic scalability
features.description: Deployable from a single Raspberry Pi to thousand-core distributed clusters.

features.title: Kubernetes operator
features.description: An official Kubernetes operator is available for deploying and managing VictoriaMetrics in cluster environments.

features.title: OpenTelemetry support
features.description: Accepts telemetry data via native OpenTelemetry integration.

features.title: Enterprise anomaly detection
features.description: The Enterprise edition adds AI/machine-learning based anomaly detection on top of the core database.

pros: Fully open source with no licensing fees for the core database
Drop-in Prometheus compatibility eases migration for existing PromQL users
Scales from tiny single-node setups to large distributed clusters
Used by large organizations including CERN, Adidas, Spotify, and Grammarly
Active open-source project with 17K+ GitHub stars and over 1 billion Docker pulls
cons: Enterprise and Cloud pricing is not publicly listed and requires contacting sales
Advanced features like anomaly detection are limited to the paid Enterprise tier
Running the open-source version at scale requires in-house operational expertise

faqs.question: Is VictoriaMetrics open source?
faqs.answer: Yes, the core VictoriaMetrics time series database is open source, with optional paid Enterprise and Cloud offerings.

faqs.question: Is VictoriaMetrics compatible with Prometheus?
faqs.answer: Yes, it is designed as a drop-in replacement for Prometheus and supports PromQL.

faqs.question: Does VictoriaMetrics offer a managed cloud option?
faqs.answer: Yes, VictoriaMetrics Cloud is offered as a fully managed SaaS deployment, though pricing is not published and requires contacting the company.

faqs.question: What else does VictoriaMetrics offer besides metrics?
faqs.answer: The VictoriaMetrics ecosystem also includes VictoriaLogs for log storage and VictoriaTraces for distributed tracing.

use_cases.title: Large-scale Kubernetes monitoring
use_cases.description: Teams running large or multi-cluster Kubernetes environments use VictoriaMetrics to collect and query metrics at high volume.

use_cases.title: Prometheus replacement
use_cases.description: Organizations replace self-managed Prometheus deployments with VictoriaMetrics for improved performance and lower storage costs.

use_cases.title: Research infrastructure monitoring
use_cases.description: Research institutions with high metric volumes use VictoriaMetrics to monitor scientific computing infrastructure.

features: Enterprise support from core engineering team

long_form_blocks.heading: What is VictoriaMetrics?
paragraphs: VictoriaMetrics is an open-source time series database and monitoring solution built for collecting, storing, and querying metrics at scale. It is designed as a drop-in replacement for Prometheus, meaning teams can keep using PromQL and existing dashboards while benefiting from its storage and query performance.

Beyond metrics, the VictoriaMetrics ecosystem includes VictoriaLogs and VictoriaTraces, extending the same underlying architecture to logs and distributed traces. The open-source core can be deployed anywhere from a Raspberry Pi to large multi-core clusters, with Enterprise and Cloud options available for organizations that need commercial support or a fully managed deployment.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who VictoriaMetrics is for
paragraphs: VictoriaMetrics is aimed at engineering and operations teams that already work with Prometheus-style metrics and need a more efficient, scalable backend. It is used by research institutions and technology companies with large-scale infrastructure monitoring needs.

enrichment.short_description: Real-time, per-second infrastructure monitoring platform with AI-powered anomaly detection and 800+ zero-configuration integrations.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Netdata: real-time per-second monitoring with AI anomaly detection. Free Community tier, plus Homelab and per-node Business/Enterprise plans.

features.title: AI-powered troubleshooting
features.description: Machine learning anomaly detection, automated root cause analysis, and blast radius identification help pinpoint issues faster.

features.title: 800+ integrations
features.description: Auto-discovery and zero-configuration monitoring across infrastructure, applications, networks, and logs.

features.title: Edge-native architecture
features.description: Distributed monitoring keeps raw data on-premises and streams only metadata to the cloud.

features.title: Lightweight agent
features.description: The monitoring agent is designed to run on roughly 5% CPU and 150MB RAM per node.

features.title: Network monitoring
features.description: Includes topology mapping, NetFlow analysis, and SNMP device monitoring.

features.title: OpenTelemetry integration
features.description: Supports metrics, logs, alerts, and OpenTelemetry data alongside native collectors.

pros: Free Community tier available with no cost for up to 5 connected nodes
Lightweight agent with a small CPU and memory footprint
Per-node pricing with no usage-based or surprise billing
SOC 2 Type 2 certified
Large open-source community with 76,000+ GitHub stars and 615+ contributors
cons: Free Community tier is capped at 5 active nodes and limited to non-commercial use
Business plan is billed per node, which can add up for large fleets
Enterprise On-Premise requires a minimum of 200 node licenses and custom pricing

faqs.question: Is Netdata free?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Community edition is free for up to 5 active connected nodes for non-commercial use.

faqs.question: How much does Netdata Business cost?
faqs.answer: The Business plan is priced at $4.50 per node per month, with a discount for annual billing.

faqs.question: Does Netdata offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, a 14-day free trial with unlimited nodes is available for the Business plan.

faqs.question: When was Netdata founded?
faqs.answer: Netdata was founded in 2016.

use_cases.title: Real-time server monitoring
use_cases.description: DevOps and SRE teams use Netdata's per-second metrics to spot and diagnose issues as they happen.

use_cases.title: Homelab monitoring
use_cases.description: Individuals running home labs can monitor unlimited nodes affordably under the Homelab plan.

use_cases.title: Air-gapped enterprise monitoring
use_cases.description: Organizations with strict data control requirements can deploy Netdata Enterprise On-Premise in air-gapped facilities.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Netdata?
paragraphs: Netdata is an open-source, edge-native infrastructure monitoring platform that collects real-time, per-second metrics without sampling or aggregation. Its lightweight agent runs directly on monitored systems, keeping raw data local while streaming only metadata to the cloud dashboard.

The platform pairs its collection layer with AI-powered troubleshooting features, including anomaly detection and automated root cause analysis, along with more than 800 integrations that auto-discover infrastructure, applications, and network devices with minimal configuration.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Netdata is for
paragraphs: Netdata serves DevOps engineers, SREs, platform engineers, system administrators, DBAs, and network engineers monitoring infrastructure of any size, from individual homelabs to enterprise environments requiring air-gapped, on-premises deployment.

enrichment.short_description: Open-source enterprise observability platform for unified monitoring of networks, servers, cloud, applications, and IoT/OT devices.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Zabbix: 100% open-source enterprise observability platform for hybrid IT and OT infrastructure, with Zabbix Cloud and paid support subscriptions.

features.title: Unified real-time monitoring
features.description: Tracks cloud services, networks, IoT/OT devices, and applications through a single interface with real-time alerting.

features.title: Log monitoring and analysis
features.description: Built-in capabilities for collecting and analyzing log data alongside metrics.

features.title: 300+ templates and integrations
features.description: Pre-built templates and integrations with third-party systems speed up onboarding of new monitoring targets.

features.title: Automated discovery and visualization
features.description: Automatically discovers infrastructure and visualizes data across the monitored environment.

features.title: Flexible deployment
features.description: Supports on-premise deployment, Zabbix Cloud hosting, and public cloud environments.

pros: 100% open source with no licensing fees for the core platform
Used across 300,000+ deployments in 190+ countries
Customer base includes large organizations such as ESA, NATO, Orange, and Dell
Fixed, predictable support pricing with no per-device charges
Fast average support response time of 12 minutes, available 24/7
cons: Zabbix Cloud and support subscription pricing are not published and require contacting sales
Self-hosted deployment requires in-house infrastructure expertise to install and maintain
Enterprise-grade support is a separate paid subscription from the free core software

faqs.question: Is Zabbix free?
faqs.answer: Yes, the core Zabbix software is 100% open source with no licensing fees.

faqs.question: Does Zabbix offer a hosted/cloud option?
faqs.answer: Yes, Zabbix Cloud is a subscription-based managed SaaS option with a free trial available.

faqs.question: Where is Zabbix based?
faqs.answer: Zabbix is headquartered in Latvia, with additional offices in the USA, Japan, Brazil, Mexico, and France.

faqs.question: When was Zabbix founded?
faqs.answer: Zabbix has been in operation since 2001.

use_cases.title: Hybrid infrastructure monitoring
use_cases.description: Enterprises with complex, multi-layered IT and OT infrastructure use Zabbix for unified monitoring across environments.

use_cases.title: Managed service provider monitoring
use_cases.description: MSPs use Zabbix to monitor infrastructure across multiple client environments from one platform.

use_cases.title: Regulated industry compliance
use_cases.description: Organizations in banking, energy, healthcare, and government use Zabbix to meet monitoring and compliance requirements.

features: Fixed pricing with no per-device charges

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Zabbix?
paragraphs: Zabbix is an open-source enterprise observability platform that unifies monitoring across IT and OT infrastructure, from cloud services and networks to IoT devices and applications, through a single interface.

The core software is 100% open source and free to self-host, while Zabbix also offers a managed Zabbix Cloud SaaS option and fixed-price technical support subscriptions for organizations that need vendor-backed assistance.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Zabbix is for
paragraphs: Zabbix is built for enterprise organizations, managed service providers, and regulated industries such as banking, energy, healthcare, and government that need scalable monitoring across complex, hybrid infrastructure.

enrichment.short_description: Open-source infrastructure monitoring platform for hybrid IT environments, with a forever-free core and paid enterprise subscriptions.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Icinga: open-source infrastructure monitoring with a forever-free edition, plus regional Repository, Module, and Support subscriptions.

features.title: Forever-free core edition
features.description: Unlimited hosts and services monitoring with no subscription required.

features.title: Infrastructure and cloud monitoring
features.description: Covers servers, networks, Kubernetes, databases, applications, Windows, and VMware across on-prem, cloud, and hybrid environments.

features.title: Integrations
features.description: Connects with Grafana, Prometheus, InfluxDB, and Elasticsearch.

features.title: Automation support
features.description: Configurable via API, Git workflows, Ansible, Puppet, and Terraform.

features.title: Enterprise modules
features.description: Paid Module Subscription unlocks additional capabilities such as Dependency Views.

features.title: Repository access
features.description: Paid Repository Subscription provides installation packages for enterprise Linux distributions like RHEL, SLES, and Amazon Linux.

pros: Core software is free forever with unlimited hosts and services
Modular subscription model lets teams pay only for the add-ons they need
60-day free trial available for the Repository Subscription
Strong automation support via API, Ansible, Puppet, and Terraform
Vendor-neutral, open-source foundation with an active community
cons: Regional pricing varies significantly (e.g., Europe/North America/Australia vs. South America/Asia/Africa)
Enterprise modules and support are billed separately from the free core software
Support Subscription starts at a relatively high annual cost (from $15,000/year in North America)

faqs.question: Is Icinga free?
faqs.answer: Yes, the core Icinga edition is free forever with unlimited hosts and services monitoring.

faqs.question: What do the paid subscriptions include?
faqs.answer: Icinga offers a Repository Subscription (enterprise Linux packages), a Module Subscription (enterprise modules like Dependency Views), and a Support Subscription (8x5 or 24/7 support).

faqs.question: Is there a free trial for paid subscriptions?
faqs.answer: Yes, a 60-day free trial is available for the Repository Subscription.

faqs.question: Does subscription pricing vary by region?
faqs.answer: Yes, Icinga's Repository, Module, and Support subscriptions are priced differently by region (Europe, North America, South America, Asia, Australia, Africa).

use_cases.title: Hybrid infrastructure monitoring
use_cases.description: Enterprises monitor on-premises, cloud, container, and Kubernetes environments through one open-source platform.

use_cases.title: Automated monitoring pipelines
use_cases.description: Teams integrate Icinga into CI/CD and infrastructure-as-code workflows using its API, Ansible, Puppet, and Terraform support.

use_cases.title: Cost-conscious enterprise monitoring
use_cases.description: Organizations that want a free core platform with optional paid support choose Icinga's modular subscription model.

features: Integrations with Grafana, Prometheus, InfluxDB, Elasticsearch
Enterprise Linux distribution packages (RHEL, SLES, Amazon Linux)
Access to enterprise modules such as Dependency Views

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Icinga?
paragraphs: Icinga is an open-source monitoring platform built for complex, hybrid IT infrastructure. Its core edition is free forever, covering unlimited hosts and services, custom checks and plugins, and integrations with tools like Grafana, Prometheus, InfluxDB, and Elasticsearch.
Beyond the free core, Icinga offers modular paid subscriptions covering enterprise Linux repository packages, additional enterprise modules, and vendor support, each priced separately and by region.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Icinga is for
paragraphs: Icinga is used by enterprises needing scalable, vendor-neutral monitoring across on-premises, cloud, and container environments, including organizations such as Adobe, Audi, Puppet, Telekom, Vodafone, and Siemens.

enrichment.short_description: Enterprise infrastructure monitoring platform with perpetual licensing, event-driven remediation, and full on-premises deployment.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Nagios XI: on-premises infrastructure monitoring with perpetual licensing, a free tier for small deployments, and Standard/Enterprise node-based pricing.

features.title: Customizable dashboards
features.description: Personalized tactical dashboards with graphs, gauges, maps, and direct device access via RDP, VNC, or SSH.

features.title: Event-driven remediation
features.description: Automatically executes scripts upon problem detection, with configurable escalation policies.

features.title: On-premises deployment
features.description: Runs entirely on-premises with no cloud dependency, supporting LDAP, Active Directory, SSO, and 2FA.

features.title: Capacity planning and SLA reporting
features.description: Enterprise tier adds performance visualization, availability tracking, and SLA reports.

features.title: Broad platform support
features.description: Monitors Windows, Linux, VMware, Hyper-V, AWS, Azure, Google Cloud, and databases including MySQL, PostgreSQL, MongoDB, and MSSQL.

features.title: Multi-channel notifications
features.description: Alerts can be routed via email, SMS, Slack, Teams, or Discord.

pros: Free tier available for small deployments (up to 7 nodes or 100 services)
Perpetual licensing model avoids ongoing subscription fees for the core license
Full on-premises deployment gives organizations complete data control
Includes 70+ configuration wizards to speed up setup
Scales to handle 15,000-20,000+ checks per 5-minute interval
cons: License cost scales quickly with node count, reaching $17,090+ for 1000-node Enterprise
Ongoing maintenance and support plans are sold separately from the perpetual license
The monitoring server itself is Linux-based, requiring a supported Linux distribution to host it

faqs.question: Is there a free version of Nagios XI?
faqs.answer: Yes, a free tier is available for up to 7 nodes or 100 services, though it does not include support.

faqs.question: Is Nagios XI a subscription or a one-time purchase?
faqs.answer: Nagios XI uses perpetual licensing based on node count, with optional annual maintenance and support plans sold separately.

faqs.question: What's the difference between Standard and Enterprise?
faqs.answer: Enterprise adds SLA reports, Business Process Intelligence, capacity planning, scheduled reports, bulk modification tools, and audit logs on top of Standard features.

faqs.question: What operating systems can host Nagios XI?
faqs.answer: Nagios XI is Linux-based, supporting distributions such as CentOS, RHEL, Ubuntu, and Debian.

use_cases.title: Small deployment monitoring
use_cases.description: Small teams monitor up to 7 nodes for free before needing a paid license.

use_cases.title: Enterprise capacity planning
use_cases.description: Larger organizations use the Enterprise tier for SLA reporting and capacity planning across hundreds of nodes.

use_cases.title: Multi-site MSP monitoring
use_cases.description: MSPs managing multiple data centers use the Sitewide plan with Nagios Fusion for multi-site connectivity.

features: Bulk modification tools and audit logs

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Nagios XI?
paragraphs: Nagios XI is an enterprise-grade network monitoring and alerting platform providing real-time visibility into servers, networks, cloud environments, and IoT devices. It runs entirely on-premises and uses perpetual, node-based licensing rather than a recurring SaaS subscription.

The platform supports event-driven remediation, capacity planning, SLA reporting, and multi-channel notifications, with plans ranging from a free tier for small deployments up to Enterprise and Sitewide licenses for large, multi-data-center environments.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Nagios XI is for
paragraphs: Nagios XI suits organizations of all sizes that want full on-premises control over their monitoring infrastructure, from small teams monitoring under 100 nodes for free to large enterprises managing hundreds or thousands of nodes across multiple data centers.

enrichment.short_description: Cloud-native observability platform using eBPF for zero-instrumentation data collection, deployed inside the customer's own cloud (BYOC).
enrichment.seo_meta_description: groundcover: eBPF-based observability platform with BYOC deployment. Free forever tier, plus per-host Pro, Enterprise, and On Premise plans.

features.title: Zero-instrumentation data collection
features.description: Uses eBPF sensors to gather telemetry across infrastructure, applications, and AI workloads without code changes.

features.title: BYOC architecture
features.description: Deploys entirely within the customer's own cloud VPC for data privacy and control.

features.title: Unified observability
features.description: Combines logs, traces, metrics, events, and real user monitoring in one platform.

features.title: AI observability
features.description: Dedicated monitoring for LLM calls and agent pipelines.

features.title: Agent Mode
features.description: An AI agent that investigates issues and can execute remediation tasks.

features.title: Migration tools
features.description: Automated tools to transition dashboards and monitors from legacy observability platforms.

pros: Free forever tier with no credit card required
BYOC deployment keeps all telemetry data inside the customer's own cloud account
Per-host pricing model avoids ingestion-based cost surprises
eBPF-based collection requires no manual code instrumentation
On Premise option available for fully self-hosted data plane and UI
cons: Free plan is limited to 12-hour data retention
Enterprise and On Premise tiers cost more per host ($35-$50/host/month)
Pricing is based on Kubernetes node counts, which requires a Kubernetes-based environment

faqs.question: Does groundcover have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, a free forever tier is available with 12-hour data retention and no credit card required.

faqs.question: How is groundcover priced?
faqs.answer: Pricing is based on the monthly average number of Kubernetes nodes monitored, starting at $30/host/month for the Pro plan.

faqs.question: What is BYOC?
faqs.answer: BYOC (Bring Your Own Cloud) means groundcover deploys entirely within the customer's own cloud environment rather than as a third-party SaaS.

faqs.question: Where is groundcover based?
faqs.answer: groundcover is headquartered in Tel Aviv, Israel, with an additional presence in California.

use_cases.title: Kubernetes observability without sampling
use_cases.description: Cloud-native teams use groundcover's eBPF sensors to get full-fidelity telemetry from Kubernetes workloads.

use_cases.title: Data residency and privacy
use_cases.description: Organizations with strict data control requirements use the BYOC model to keep telemetry inside their own cloud account.

use_cases.title: AI workload monitoring
use_cases.description: Teams running LLM and agent pipelines use groundcover's AI observability features to monitor those workloads alongside infrastructure.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is groundcover?
paragraphs: groundcover is a cloud-native observability platform that uses eBPF sensors to collect telemetry data without requiring manual code instrumentation. Instead of operating as a typical third-party SaaS, groundcover deploys its data plane inside the customer's own cloud environment (BYOC), keeping observability data under the customer's control.
The platform unifies logs, traces, metrics, events, and real user monitoring, and includes AI observability features for monitoring LLM calls and agent pipelines alongside traditional infrastructure.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who groundcover is for
paragraphs: groundcover is built for engineering teams running cloud-native, Kubernetes-based infrastructure who want full-fidelity telemetry, predictable per-host pricing, and control over where their observability data lives.

enrichment.short_description: AI-native observability platform with index-free querying, infinite retention in customer-owned storage, and unit-based pricing.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Coralogix: AI-native observability platform with infinite retention and unit-based pricing for logs, traces, metrics, and AI workloads.

features.title: Index-free querying
features.description: Remote querying across all data types using a unified syntax, without needing to pre-index data.

features.title: Infinite retention
features.description: Stores data in the customer's own cloud environment (S3) for long-term, high-cardinality retention.

features.title: DataPrime engine
features.description: A query engine for cross-signal correlation and unified data exploration.

features.title: AI interfaces
features.description: Includes Olly, a conversational AI interface, Model Context Protocol (MCP) support, and a programmable CLI.

features.title: AI observability modules
features.description: Dedicated Discovery, Guardrails, cost tracking, and session explorer modules for monitoring AI systems.

features.title: Cost Optimizer
features.description: Reduces costs by intelligently tiering data while preserving visibility.

features.title: Security capabilities
features.description: Includes SIEM and managed detection and response (MDR) functionality.

pros: Unit-based pricing means paying only for the data volume actually used, rather than fixed tiers
Infinite retention with data stored in the customer's own S3 bucket
14-day free trial with full feature access and no credit card required
Compliance certifications including SOC 2, PCI DSS, ISO 27001, GDPR, and HIPAA
Named a Gartner Magic Quadrant Leader for Observability Platforms in 2026
cons: Consumption-based pricing can be harder to predict than flat-rate plans
Logs, traces, metrics, and AI usage are billed separately by unit type
Achieving the full retention cost benefit requires managing a customer-owned S3 bucket

faqs.question: How is Coralogix priced?
faqs.answer: Coralogix uses unit-based pricing: $0.42/GB for logs, $0.16/GB for traces, $0.05/GB for metrics, and $1.50 per 1M tokens for AI usage.

faqs.question: Is there a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, Coralogix offers a 14-day free trial with full feature access and 8 units of quota, no credit card required.

faqs.question: What compliance certifications does Coralogix have?
faqs.answer: Coralogix is SOC 2, PCI DSS, ISO 27001, GDPR, and HIPAA compliant.

faqs.question: When was Coralogix founded?
faqs.answer: Coralogix was founded in 2014.

use_cases.title: Long-term log retention
use_cases.description: Organizations needing infinite log and trace retention without escalating storage costs use Coralogix's customer-owned S3 storage model.

use_cases.title: Regulated industry observability
use_cases.description: Fintech and healthcare organizations use Coralogix's compliance certifications to meet regulatory requirements.

use_cases.title: AI system monitoring
use_cases.description: Teams building AI-driven applications use Coralogix's AI observability modules to monitor discovery, guardrails, and cost.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Coralogix?
paragraphs: Coralogix is an observability platform built for AI-driven infrastructure, offering index-free querying across logs, traces, and metrics, and infinite data retention by storing telemetry in the customer's own cloud environment.

Founded in 2014, the platform has grown to serve over 4,000 enterprise teams and includes AI-native features such as Olly (a conversational AI interface), MCP support, and dedicated AI observability modules for monitoring AI systems.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Coralogix is for
paragraphs: Coralogix targets enterprise engineering teams across fintech, healthcare, gaming, video streaming, ecommerce, and cybersecurity that need compliance-certified observability with long-term data retention and predictable, consumption-based pricing.

enrichment.short_description: Cloud-based observability platform unifying log management, infrastructure monitoring, and distributed tracing with an AI agent for root cause analysis.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Logz.io: unified log management, infrastructure monitoring, and tracing platform with an AI agent for root cause analysis. 14-day free trial.

features.title: OrionIQ AI agent
features.description: A workflow-aware AI agent that automates root cause analysis and answers questions in natural language.

features.title: Open 360 platform
features.description: A unified dashboard combining logs, metrics, and traces with real-time insights.

features.title: Log management
features.description: Positioned as a managed alternative to self-hosting the ELK Stack.

features.title: Infrastructure monitoring
features.description: Positioned as a managed alternative to self-hosting Prometheus.

features.title: Distributed tracing
features.description: Tracks requests across distributed services.

features.title: 300+ integrations
features.description: Supports AWS, Azure, Google Cloud, Kubernetes, Docker, MongoDB, PostgreSQL, and other common infrastructure tools.

features.title: Cost management tools
features.description: Data optimization features aimed at reducing telemetry costs.

pros: 14-day free trial with no credit card required
Unifies logs, metrics, and traces in a single Open 360 dashboard
OrionIQ AI agent automates root cause analysis and natural language querying
300+ built-in integrations across major cloud and infrastructure tools
Recognized by AWS across multiple competencies, including Generative AI Software
cons: Consumption-based pricing means costs scale directly with data volume
Monthly billing costs 1.2x the equivalent annual rate
Overage beyond committed volume is charged at 1.4x the standard subscription rate
Detailed plan tiers are not published and require a demo or quote

faqs.question: Does Logz.io offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, Logz.io offers a 14-day free trial with no credit card required.

faqs.question: How is Logz.io priced?
faqs.answer: Pricing is consumption-based: $0.92/GB per day for Log Management, $0.40 per 1,000 time-series metrics per day for Infrastructure Monitoring, and $0.16 per 1M spans per day for Distributed Tracing.

faqs.question: What does OrionIQ do?
faqs.answer: OrionIQ is Logz.io's AI agent that automates root cause analysis and lets users query data in natural language.

faqs.question: When was Logz.io founded?
faqs.answer: Logz.io was founded in 2015, originally as Logshero Ltd.

use_cases.title: Replacing self-managed ELK/Prometheus
use_cases.description: Teams running the ELK Stack or Prometheus themselves migrate to Logz.io for a managed alternative.

use_cases.title: Unified observability
use_cases.description: Organizations wanting logs, metrics, and traces in one dashboard use the Open 360 platform.

use_cases.title: AI-assisted incident response
use_cases.description: Teams use OrionIQ to speed up root cause analysis during incidents.

features: 6 data points per minute per time series

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Logz.io?
paragraphs: Logz.io is a cloud-based observability platform, founded in 2015, that unifies log management, infrastructure monitoring, and distributed tracing into a single interface called Open 360. It positions itself as a managed alternative to self-hosting tools like the ELK Stack and Prometheus.
The platform includes OrionIQ, a workflow-aware AI agent that automates root cause analysis and lets teams query their observability data using natural language.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Logz.io is for
paragraphs: Logz.io is aimed at DevOps teams, SREs, and engineering organizations that want to streamline incident response and reduce the operational overhead of running open-source observability tools themselves.

enrichment.short_description: Telemetry pipeline platform that ingests, transforms, and routes logs, metrics, and traces, paired with an AI agent for root cause analysis.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Mezmo: telemetry pipeline platform for real-time log, metric, and trace processing, with an included AI agent for root cause analysis.

features.title: Active Telemetry pipeline
features.description: Processes logs, metrics, and traces in real time before they reach downstream observability tools.

features.title: Live tail and replay
features.description: Enables real-time streaming and replay of telemetry data for incident investigation.

features.title: Tail-based sampling
features.description: Captures critical errors while reducing the volume of routine telemetry data.

features.title: Data profiling
features.description: Identifies high-volume, low-value data streams for optimization.

features.title: Pipeline-as-code
features.description: Supports defining telemetry pipelines as code for CI/CD integration.

features.title: Cardinality management
features.description: Aggregates and manages metric cardinality to help control observability costs.

features.title: Agentic root cause analysis (AURA)
features.description: An open-source agentic harness where AI agents investigate infrastructure incidents with human-in-the-loop review before risky actions are taken.

pros: Telemetry pipeline filters and transforms data before it reaches costly downstream observability tools, which one customer used to cut Datadog costs by 52%
AI root cause analysis agent is included in the platform license with no separate metered query charges
Pipeline-as-code support integrates with CI/CD workflows
Human-in-the-loop review keeps risky automated actions reviewable before they touch production
The AURA agentic component is open source
cons: Specific pricing tiers and a starting price are not published on the website
Configuring pipeline routing and transformation rules requires understanding telemetry pipeline concepts
Free access is limited to a trial rather than an ongoing free tier

faqs.question: What is Mezmo's Active Telemetry pipeline?
faqs.answer: It is a real-time engine that ingests, transforms, and routes logs, metrics, and traces before they reach observability tools or storage.

faqs.question: Does Mezmo charge extra for AI features?
faqs.answer: No, according to Mezmo the AI root cause analysis agent is included in the platform license with no metered query charges.

faqs.question: Is there a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, a free trial is available directly on Mezmo's website.

faqs.question: What is AURA?
faqs.answer: AURA is Mezmo's open-source agentic harness that lets AI agents investigate and help resolve production infrastructure incidents with human oversight.

use_cases.title: Observability cost reduction
use_cases.description: Teams use Mezmo's telemetry pipeline to filter and transform data before it reaches costly downstream tools like Datadog.
use_cases.audience: SRE and platform teams managing observability spend

use_cases.title: AI-assisted incident investigation
use_cases.description: SRE teams use Mezmo's agentic capabilities to get AI-driven investigation of incidents while retaining human review of risky actions.

use_cases.title: Pipeline automation
use_cases.description: Engineering teams define and deploy telemetry pipeline configurations as code within CI/CD workflows.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Mezmo?
paragraphs: Mezmo is a telemetry pipeline platform built around what the company calls an Active Telemetry approach: processing logs, metrics, and traces in real time before they reach observability tools, rather than shipping raw data downstream and paying to store and query all of it.

The platform includes AURA, an open-source agentic harness that lets AI agents investigate production infrastructure incidents using real telemetry, runbooks, and incident history, with human-in-the-loop review required before any risky remediation actions are taken.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Mezmo is for
paragraphs: Mezmo is aimed at site reliability engineers, platform engineers, and developers at organizations looking to control observability costs and speed up incident response using AI-assisted, but human-supervised, investigation.

enrichment.short_description: Observability platform for distributed tracing, log analytics, and AI/LLM workflow debugging, with a free tier for individual projects.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Honeycomb: observability platform for distributed tracing and AI workflow debugging. Free tier up to 20M events/month, Pro from $150/month.

features.title: Distributed tracing
features.description: Tracks requests across microservices to understand system behavior in production.

features.title: BubbleUp
features.description: Automated root cause analysis that surfaces the dimensions most correlated with an anomaly.

features.title: Honeycomb Intelligence
features.description: AI-powered investigation tools including Canvas, an AI copilot, plus Model Context Protocol (MCP) integration.

features.title: Agent Timeline and LLM Observability
features.description: Purpose-built views for debugging AI agent and LLM-powered workflows.

features.title: Service Map
features.description: Visualizes system architecture and service dependencies.

features.title: SLO tracking
features.description: Defines and monitors service level objectives.

features.title: 60+ integrations
features.description: Connects with CI/CD, incident management, and other development tools.

pros: Free tier available with up to 20M events and 100M metrics data points per month
Query results returned in under 90 seconds, with sub-10-second queries using AI agents
OpenTelemetry-native platform
Used by well-known engineering teams including Slack, Intercom, Dropbox, Booking.com, and Duolingo
Company has published the book "Observability Engineering"
cons: Pro plan pricing starts at $150/month once free-tier limits are exceeded
Advanced features like SSO, Agent Timeline, and SLOs require the paid Pro tier or above
Enterprise pricing is custom and requires a quote

faqs.question: Does Honeycomb have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, a free plan supports up to 20M events and 100M metrics data points per month.

faqs.question: What's included in the free tier?
faqs.answer: The free tier includes 2 triggers, distributed tracing, BubbleUp, OpenTelemetry support, the Canvas AI copilot, and Honeycomb MCP.

faqs.question: What is BubbleUp?
faqs.answer: BubbleUp is Honeycomb's automated root cause analysis feature that highlights the dimensions most correlated with an anomaly.

faqs.question: Does Honeycomb support OpenTelemetry?
faqs.answer: Yes, Honeycomb is built as an OpenTelemetry-native platform.

use_cases.title: Individual and small project monitoring
use_cases.description: Developers use the free tier to add observability to individual projects or small applications.

use_cases.title: Production tracing at scale
use_cases.description: Teams running production applications use the Pro plan for higher event volumes, SLOs, and SSO.

use_cases.title: AI agent debugging
use_cases.description: Teams building AI-powered applications use Agent Timeline and LLM Observability to debug agent workflows.

features: Distributed tracing, BubbleUp, Canvas AI Copilot, Honeycomb MCP
Query Data API and AWS PrivateLink

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Honeycomb?
paragraphs: Honeycomb is an observability platform that helps engineering teams monitor and debug software systems in production, built around distributed tracing, log analytics, and time series metrics on an OpenTelemetry-native foundation.

The platform has expanded to include AI-focused capabilities such as Agent Timeline and LLM Observability for debugging AI agent workflows, along with Honeycomb Intelligence tools like the Canvas AI copilot and Model Context Protocol (MCP) integration.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Honeycomb is for
paragraphs: Honeycomb serves individual developers testing projects on its free tier, as well as engineering teams at companies like Slack, Intercom, Dropbox, and Duolingo running production distributed systems and, increasingly, AI/LLM-powered applications.
